page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, the instance consists of 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations reveal strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core and memory throughput limits. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded tasks, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2258.0, single-threaded gzip compression at 35.11 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.8 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core and uncached memory operations fall into lower performance brackets. The multi-core Geekbench score is 5040.0, while the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is 3485.37. Uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths are limited to 32.39 GB/sec and 20.69 GB/sec respectively, which impacts data-intensive operations like software compilation and database transactions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio within a dedicated hardware profile, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive multi-threaded compute power. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing pipelines, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference, where its AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets can be leveraged. However, it is less optimal for highly parallelized workloads, large-scale database operations, or intensive software compilation due to its lower multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM, powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show exceptional single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2258.0 and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as seen in its multi-core stress-ng and Passmark database scores. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive, single-threaded tasks, text processing, and light LLM inference, while presenting a clear tradeoff for highly parallelized or database-heavy workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It excels in single-threaded tasks but shows lower multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs and 31 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance delivering strong single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for specialized, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM providing 7.75 GB per core
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
Top-tier single-threaded performance in compression and text processing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for memory-bound, single-threaded tasks and light LLM inference
